text:
Market today: Semiconductors are struggling, and many tech stocks are down significantly in July, even as the broader market shows health. The rise of Chinese AI models adds a new competitive layer to your concentrated tech/chip book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-46.77%) and SPAL (-47.83%) are your biggest concerns due to their leveraged nature and massive drawdowns; margin amplifies these drops. SMH (-12.55%), QQQ (-2.7%), and QQQI (-3.9%) are also flagged as "SELL" and nearing their stops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on your semiconductor exposure given the current struggle. Also, monitor the impact of increased Chinese AI competition on US tech sentiment.

Bottom line: Your high concentration in struggling tech/chips, especially leveraged ones, demands strict stop-loss discipline and risk re-evaluation.
plays: Respect stops on QQQ (670), SMH (540), and QQQI (52) as they are flagged "SELL" and nearing levels.
Re-evaluate SOXL and SPAL stops (200, 21.38 respectively) given their leveraged nature and deep drawdowns; these default stops may be too wide for current prices.
Consider watching SOXS (inverse semiconductor ETF) given the sector's struggle. Scanner shows SOXS WATCH, entry 52.77-52.27, stop 51.07.
